methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $36M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Hystar is a Series B climate and energy technology company that has raised $36M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of nine private peers and one public comparable ( Linza, ticker LNZA) operating in the same sector and stage. The private peer set includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), reflecting substantial dispersion in the sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $36M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $68M. Peer-median analysis anchors to the six same-sector, same-stage comparable valuations, which have a median of $355M and span a range from $120M to $5.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity within frontier energy.

The two methods produce a triangulated valuation range of $58M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $212M. The stage-progression method implies conservatively lower valuations, while peer medians reflect the substantial capital requirements and risk profiles of climate-tech development. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation within the crowded sector, path to commercialization, and capital efficiency relative to peers. Resolution of these factors will likely drive valuation within the derived range.
